cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out what the main idea of this passage is. Let me read through it again. The passage starts by talking about how both women and men are surrounded by media that focuses on diet, exercise, fashion, and hairstyles. It mentions that men don't just need to wear a suit to be considered smart and handsome anymore. Then there's a survey from the University of the West of England that says 80% of men are unhappy with their bodies, specifically mentioning beer bellies and lack of muscles. The passage discusses the influence of role models in TV and magazines, leading to a perception of a perfect body. It mentions a muscle-enhancing shirt as a product for men, but Joe Warner, a former editor, calls it a gimmick and advises more exercise. The conclusion is that exercise is good,
